rationale: First and only US-listed pure-play quantum computing company; quantum is explicitly named in the profile as a next wave and the watchlist currently has zero quantum exposure.

Established robotic-surgery franchise with a fresh 10-million-procedure milestone confirming structural adoption; adds a profitable robotics pure-play to complement the broader BOTZ and ARKQ thematic ETFs the watchlist already holds.

Pure-play SMR exposure to back the user's near-term fission thesis; NuScale is the only US developer with NRC design certification and the AI/data-center power-demand narrative is gathering steam in summer 2023 financial press.

Broaden the under-represented aging-population/healthcare wave beyond a single robotic-surgery name (ISRG); XLV is the cheapest, most liquid US large-cap healthcare basket and captures the GLP-1 obesity-drug breakout already visible by mid-2023.
Zepbound (tirzepatide) FDA-approved for obesity on Nov 8, 2023 opens a multi-decade aging/metabolic-disease commercial runway that maps directly onto the profile's aging-demographics non-tech wave.

NuScale terminated its flagship UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project on Nov 8, 2023 after subscription targets failed; this is a fundamental thesis breach for the only pure-play US SMR equity and the broader nuclear thesis is still expressible via URA, CCJ, and XLE.

Closes the obvious hyperscaler gap in the watchlist: AWS is the third pillar of the AI-infrastructure buildout alongside MSFT-Azure and GOOGL-GCP, and Amazon just completed its $4B Anthropic investment on March 27, 2024, locking Anthropic to AWS Trainium/Inferentia and Bedrock.

Most redundant healthcare exposure on the list now that LLY (GLP-1 leader, $2B Mounjaro+Zepbound in Q1 2024) carries the aging-demographics thesis and ISRG covers medical robotics; XLV is a broad sector ETF that dilutes both signals and frees a slot under the 16-cap for the missing AI hyperscaler.

TSMC is the sole-source fab behind every leading-edge AI accelerator; Q1 FY25 Nvidia data center revenue at $22.6B (+427% YoY) and reports that NVDA/AMD/AVGO have locked up TSMC's 3nm capacity make TSM the picks-and-shovels exposure missing from the current AI cluster.

Constellation Energy signed a 20-year PPA with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1, the first concrete hyperscaler-to-nuclear deal and a clear buildup-phase signal for the SMR/nuclear-revival wave named in the profile.

AAPL has lagged the 2024 AI mega-cap trade (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AMZN already cover that exposure) and the iPhone 16 / Apple Intelligence launch drew muted demand signals; consistent with the profile's 'trim before crest' discipline on AI, drop the weakest AI mega-cap to free a slot for the emerging nuclear leg.

NuScale is the only US SMR vendor with NRC design approval (uprated 77 MWe module cleared in 2024) and announced a TVA deployment agreement covering up to 6 GW across seven states, making it a near-term SMR pure-play that complements CCJ (uranium fuel cycle) and CEG (operating reactors plus hyperscaler PPAs).

Vertiv is the picks-and-shovels play on AI datacenter power and liquid cooling, with rack densities now exceeding 20 kW driving demand for its direct-to-chip CDUs, busbar, and UPS; complements our hyperscaler-and-chip AI exposure (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/NVDA/TSM) with infrastructure leverage.

GLP-1 revenue guidance miss in Q4 2024 triggered a ~21% three-month decline; trailing P/E still near 80x after the sell-off marks a peak-enthusiasm valuation breach for the demographics/obesity-drug thesis just as NVO competition and supply normalization weigh on growth.

Palantir's Q4-2024 results showed a clear commercial inflection (US commercial +54% YoY, US gov +45% YoY) and the company is the cleanest public bet on AI applied to defense/intelligence software, fitting both the AI and geopolitical waves the profile emphasizes.

Robotics is named as a 'next wave' in the profile but the watchlist only carries ISRG (medical surgical robotics); Q2-25 saw the broader humanoid/industrial robotics theme inflect with Figure AI's $40B-valuation round and Tesla's Optimus production announcements, and ROBO is the cleanest US-listed pure-play robotics ETF to fill that thematic gap.

Apple SVP Eddy Cue's May 2025 antitrust testimony disclosed that Google search volume in Safari declined for the first time, attributed to users shifting to AI alternatives; combined with the ongoing DOJ remedies phase (potential Chrome/search-distribution remedies), the original 'Google search moat' thesis is showing genuine erosion, while AI exposure is preserved via MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, TSM, and PLTR.

Oracle's September 9, 2025 fiscal Q1-26 print disclosed a step-change in remaining performance obligations (RPO ~$455B, up ~4x year over year) driven by multi-year OCI capacity contracts with frontier AI labs; this is a concrete commercial milestone that moves Oracle from a legacy database name into a top-tier AI-infrastructure beneficiary alongside the watchlist's existing NVDA/MSFT/AMZN/TSM exposure.

Palantir is the clearest peak-enthusiasm signal on the current watchlist: by late September 2025 the stock traded at roughly 90-100x forward sales (the highest multiple in the large-cap software universe), sell-side price targets had compressed below spot, and short interest plus insider selling had risen materially - classic late-stage signals the mean-variance optimizer's covariance estimate will not price in; AI exposure is preserved via MSFT/NVDA/AMZN/TSM and the incoming ORCL.

Quantum hardware moved from research curiosity to investable wave in Q4 2025 (IBM Nighthawk 120-qubit processor delivered Nov 12, Google Willow chip demoed 'below threshold' error correction in December, Wedbush initiating Outperform coverage on the pure-play quantum names); IONQ alone is too thin a thematic foothold given the catalyst density, and Rigetti adds a complementary superconducting-architecture exposure (vs IONQ's trapped-ion) at a roadmap that targets 100+ qubit chiplet systems by end of 2025.

Pure wave-bucket housekeeping at the 16-name cap: AI currently holds five tickers (MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, TSM, ORCL), exceeding the curator's max-4-per-bucket discipline; AMZN is the most diluted AI play of the five (a third of its earnings come from retail/logistics rather than AI infrastructure), so its removal preserves all the focused AI-infrastructure exposure - hyperscaler (MSFT, ORCL), GPU (NVDA), foundry (TSM) - while opening a slot for the quantum add and bringing AI back to the 4-name cap.
